quickstart.running_at: Koskidex is now running at http://localhost:7700

docker_compose.title: Add to your Docker Compose
docker_compose.desc: Add Koskidex as a service alongside your existing application. Works with any stack.
docker_compose.add_service: Add the service to your docker-compose.yml:
docker_compose.env_vars: Then set the environment variable in your app to connect:
docker_compose.sail_desc: If you use Laravel Sail, add the koskidex service to your docker-compose.yml and reference it in your .env:

php.title: PHP / Laravel
php.desc: Full integration with Eloquent models and automatic sync on save/update/delete.
php.sync_note: Models are automatically synced with Koskidex on every save(), update() and delete(). No workers needed.

js.title: JavaScript / Node.js
js.desc: Lightweight client for Node.js and browser environments.

python.title: Python
python.desc: Simple client using requests. Works with Django, Flask, FastAPI, or standalone.

api.title: REST API Reference
api.desc: Koskidex exposes a simple REST API. Use it from any language with HTTP support.
api.auth_desc: If started with --api-key, all requests require a Bearer token:

architecture.title: Architecture & Concepts
architecture.desc: Understand how Koskidex works under the hood.

concepts.indices_desc: An index is a collection of similar documents. Each index has its own settings, synonyms, and ranking rules.
concepts.documents_desc: Documents are JSON objects. Koskidex is schemaless — every document only needs a unique 'id' field.
concepts.search_desc: Inverted index with Damerau-Levenshtein fuzzy matching, bigram prefix indexing, field weighting, dynamic fuzziness, explicit sorting, faceted search, geospatial filtering, and hybrid vector search.
concepts.storage_desc: GOB binary snapshots with a Write-Ahead Log (WAL) for crash recovery. Every mutation is appended to the WAL before returning, ensuring zero data loss.

search_status.offline_desc: Cannot connect to the search service at {{url}}.

error.something_wrong: Something went wrong in the search engine UI. Don't worry, it's not you, it's us.
